title: Autonomous Development Skills Suite: Six Composable Skills for AI Agents
description:
You are using an AI agent that can do things you cannot fully verify. You are still responsible for the outcome.

Two stances are common, and both are wrong. “I will review everything” does not scale — by the time it breaks down, you are rubber-stamping. “The agent is good enough” is not a property of the agent — it is a property of the agent on this task, this codebase, this day. You do not know in advance which runs are the ones where it costs you.

The question neither stance answers: what does it take for a human to safely hand real work to an AI more capable than themselves — and remain the responsible party for what gets done?

If an autonomous action cannot be traced, it is broken. Someone is accountable for that agent. They must be able to audit every decision it made and the reasoning behind it. If the reasoning is invisible, accountability is a fantasy.

Three principles govern this suite — not guidelines, architectural constraints:

  1. Commander’s Intent — Define the destination. Never prescribe the route. An agent following a checklist misses everything the checklist did not mention. The checklist becomes the ceiling.
  2. Observable Autonomy — Autonomy is a function of transparency. Every autonomous operation must produce a visible, continuous trail — not on request, not as a post-hoc summary, but as a structural property of the operation itself. Autonomy without observability is not delegation — it is abdication.
  3. Convergence Is Silence — The system has converged when diverse independent evaluators find nothing left to change. Not when a score stops moving.

These six skills are the implementation of those three principles:

  1. Hunch - surfaces the agent’s guesses about where the operator is heading and turns them into falsifiable questions. Closes the gap between explicit vision and implicit direction before the loop starts.
  2. Intent - states the agent’s interpretation of the ask before any work begins. If it misread you, you see it before it acts.
  3. Improve - one highest-leverage change per run, stated reason, recorded. The core autonomous loop.
  4. Probe - paired cases that look similar but differ in one material way. If the agent’s response does not change, you have evidence of pattern-matching, not reasoning.
  5. Trail - appends a structured, multi-resolution evidence entry to .trail/log.md after every session. Nothing is lost. Every decision and its rationale are on record.
  6. Retrospect - reads the full trail as a single document and forms arc-level claims about where the work actually is and whether the loop’s attention is in the right place.

Evidence status. Convergence reached: three independent silence runs from distinct model families. The suite has been applied to its own source more than 100 times. It restructured itself twice.

Canonical source: GitHub repository. Companion theory: Autonomous Agent Principles on Zenodo.
